name: Classic Meatloaf
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound ground pork butt or sweet sausage meat
1/3 cup grated Romano or Parmesan cheese or sharp cheddar cheese
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the bread crumbs in milk.
2. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (232 degrees C).
3. Lightly grease a loaf pan.
4. Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat until hot.
5. Add the onions to the skillet and saute for about 8 minutes or until golden brown, stirring occasionally.
6. Add the garlic and continue cooking for a few seconds.
7. Remove the skillet from the heat and transfer the contents (onions and garlic) to a mixing bowl.
8. Add the soaked bread crumbs, ground beef chuck, and ground pork butt to the bowl with onions. Mix in 1 egg, grated cheese, fennel seeds, oregano, and chopped parsley. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Taste the mixture before filling the loaf pan, adjusting seasoning if necessary.
10. Transfer the meatloaf mixture to the prepared loaf pan and bake at 450 degrees F (232 degrees C) for 15 minutes.
11. Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
12. Bake the meatloaf for an additional hour or until the internal temperature reaches 165 degrees F (74 degrees C).
13. Allow the meatloaf to rest for 15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Yield: 6 servings
